articles.title: Free Fenway Franks? Diner rolls out Opening Day deal for Red Sox fans.
articles.description:
South Street Diner will offer all-you-can-eat hot dogs with a drink purchase during the home opener on April 3.

The post Free Fenway Franks? Diner rolls out Opening Day deal for Red Sox fans. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: South Street Diner offers all-you-can-eat hot dogs on April 3 for Red Sox home opener.
Deal requires a drink purchase, enhancing the game day experience for fans.
Local residents can enjoy a budget-friendly meal while celebrating the start of the baseball season.

articles.title: Boston Marathon runner honors brother lost to suicide, supports fellow veterans
articles.description:
After losing her brother to suicide, Heather Diamond is running for Home Base, a joint initiative of Massachusetts General Hospital and The Red Sox Foundation.

The post Boston Marathon runner honors brother lost to suicide, supports fellow veterans appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Heather Diamond runs the Boston Marathon to honor her brother lost to suicide.
She supports Home Base, a program by Massachusetts General Hospital and The Red Sox Foundation.
Her efforts aim to raise awareness and aid fellow veterans in Boston.

articles.title: Aerin Frankel posts PWHL single-season record sixth shutout as Fleet blank Goldeneyes
articles.description:
The “Fran-kel, Fran-kel” chants at Tsongas Center eventually gave way to a familiar “M-V-P” refrain by the middle of the third period.

The post Aerin Frankel posts PWHL single-season record sixth shutout as Fleet blank Goldeneyes appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Aerin Frankel achieved a PWHL record with her sixth shutout on March 25.
The Fleet defeated the Goldeneyes, enhancing their playoff positioning.
Local fans celebrated Frankel's performance, boosting community support for women's hockey.
